mnemonics: Think of Ethereum as a global computer (GPC) that runs on Gas: Gas Powers Computations.
keyPoints: Ethereum is a decentralized, open-source blockchain with smart contract functionality.
It uses a cryptocurrency called Ether (ETH) to pay for transaction fees and computational services (gas).
The network is transitioning from Proof-of-Work (PoW) to Proof-of-Stake (PoS) consensus via 'The Merge'.
commonMistakes: Believing gas fees are paid in 'Ethereum' instead of 'Ether' (ETH).
Thinking smart contracts are legal agreements rather than self-executing code stored on the blockchain.
